syllabus: IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches HL

topics.name: Sequences & Series
topics.objectives: Understand arithmetic and geometric sequences and series; use sigma notation; determine convergence of infinite geometric series; find Maclaurin expansions.
topics.common_mistakes: Confusing common difference and common ratio; forgetting the condition |r| < 1 for convergence; off-by-one errors in sigma notation limits.
topics.remediation: Start with pattern recognition in sequences before introducing formal notation. Practise identifying whether a series converges before calculating the sum.

topics.name: Exponents & Logarithms
topics.objectives: Apply laws of exponents and logarithms fluently; convert between forms; solve exponential and logarithmic equations.
topics.common_mistakes: Applying log(a+b) = log(a) + log(b) incorrectly; forgetting domain restrictions (argument must be positive); errors with change of base.
topics.remediation: Emphasise that log laws mirror exponent laws. Use numerical examples to build intuition. Always check domain validity of solutions.

topics.name: Binomial Theorem
subtopics: Expansion of (a+b)^n for positive integer n
General term and specific term finding
Extension to fractional and negative indices (AHL)
topics.objectives: Expand binomial expressions using the Binomial Theorem; find specific terms; extend to fractional/negative indices at HL.
topics.common_mistakes: Off-by-one errors in the general term index; forgetting to apply the power to both parts of each term; sign errors when b is negative.
topics.remediation: Write out the general term formula explicitly each time. Start with Pascal's triangle for small n before transitioning to the formula.

topics.name: Proofs
topics.objectives: Construct formal proofs using induction, contradiction, and counterexample; write clear logical arguments.
topics.common_mistakes: Incomplete induction step (not using the inductive hypothesis); failing to state the base case; assuming what you want to prove in contradiction.
topics.remediation: Use a structured template: base case → inductive hypothesis → inductive step → conclusion. Practise each proof type separately.

topics.name: Complex Numbers
subtopics: Roots of polynomials with complex coefficients
topics.objectives: Perform arithmetic with complex numbers; convert between forms; apply De Moivre's theorem; find nth roots of complex numbers.
topics.common_mistakes: Sign errors when multiplying complex numbers; confusing argument conventions; forgetting all n roots in nth root problems.
topics.remediation: Visualise on the Argand diagram. Practise converting between Cartesian and polar forms before tackling De Moivre.

topics.name: Systems of Equations
subtopics: Systems of up to 3 linear equations
topics.objectives: Solve systems of linear equations using row reduction; classify solution types; interpret geometrically.
topics.common_mistakes: Arithmetic errors in row operations; misinterpreting dependent systems as inconsistent; not parameterising infinite solutions.
topics.remediation: Use systematic row reduction and always verify by substituting back. Draw 3D sketches for geometric interpretation.

topics.name: Functions Basics
topics.objectives: Understand function notation; determine domain and range; form composites and inverses; use technology to explore graphs.
topics.common_mistakes: Confusing f(g(x)) with g(f(x)); forgetting to restrict domain for inverse to exist; not stating domain/range explicitly.
topics.remediation: Use mapping diagrams and graphical approaches to build conceptual understanding before algebraic methods.

topics.name: Quadratics
subtopics: Axis of symmetry and vertex form
topics.objectives: Solve quadratic equations using multiple methods; interpret the discriminant; solve quadratic inequalities.
topics.common_mistakes: Sign errors when completing the square; forgetting ± in the quadratic formula; reversing inequality direction.
topics.remediation: Practise all methods on the same equation. Use graphical approach to connect the discriminant to the parabola's position.

topics.name: Rational, Exponential & Log Functions
topics.objectives: Model with exponential, logarithmic, and rational functions; identify asymptotes; solve equations graphically and algebraically.
topics.common_mistakes: Confusing horizontal and vertical asymptotes; misidentifying growth vs decay; forgetting asymptotes of rational functions.
topics.remediation: Sketch by hand identifying key features (intercepts, asymptotes) first, then verify with technology.

topics.name: Transformations
topics.objectives: Apply and describe transformations of functions; combine multiple transformations; relate to function notation.
topics.common_mistakes: Getting the direction of horizontal shifts wrong (f(x-a) shifts right); mixing up stretch factors.
topics.remediation: Start with a simple function and apply one transformation at a time. Compare the equation to the graph each time.

topics.name: Functions (AHL)
topics.objectives: Classify functions as odd/even; apply factor and remainder theorems; decompose into partial fractions; solve polynomial inequalities.
topics.common_mistakes: Forgetting to check both conditions for even/odd; wrong partial fraction form for repeated factors; sign errors in polynomial division.
topics.remediation: Practise the factor theorem to find roots systematically. Use the cover-up method for partial fractions where applicable.

topics.name: Measurement & Trigonometry
subtopics: Area of a triangle (½ ab sin C)
topics.objectives: Apply sine and cosine rules; work with radians; calculate arc lengths and sector areas; solve 3D geometry problems.
topics.common_mistakes: Using degrees instead of radians; applying sine rule for the ambiguous case incorrectly; 3D angle identification errors.
topics.remediation: Always verify calculator mode. Draw clear diagrams for 3D problems to identify the right triangle.

topics.name: Unit Circle & Identities
topics.objectives: Use the unit circle; recall exact values; apply Pythagorean and double angle identities; solve trig equations.
topics.common_mistakes: Not finding all solutions in a given interval; misapplying double angle formulae; forgetting related identities (1 + tan²θ = sec²θ).
topics.remediation: Use the unit circle systematically. Always check how many solutions exist in the given domain.

topics.name: Circular Functions
subtopics: Graphs of sin, cos, and tan
topics.objectives: Sketch and transform trig graphs; determine amplitude, period, and phase shift; model periodic phenomena.
topics.common_mistakes: Errors reading period from transformed equations; confusing phase shift direction; wrong vertical shift.
topics.remediation: Start from the parent function and apply transformations step by step. Verify with technology.

topics.name: Vectors
topics.objectives: Perform vector operations; find scalar products and angles; write vector equations of lines; determine intersections.
topics.common_mistakes: Sign errors in vector subtraction; confusing position vectors with direction vectors; not checking for skew lines.
topics.remediation: Draw vector diagrams before computing. Practise scalar product calculations methodically.

topics.name: Trig Identities (AHL)
subtopics: Compound angle formulae sin(A±B), cos(A±B), tan(A±B)
topics.objectives: Derive and apply compound angle formulae; use inverse trig functions with correct domains; prove identities.
topics.common_mistakes: Misapplying compound angle vs double angle formulae; wrong domain for inverse trig; confusing sin(A+B) with sinA + sinB.
topics.remediation: Derive double angle from compound angle each time. Practise working with restricted domains for inverse functions.

topics.name: Vector Applications
topics.objectives: Compute cross products; find equations of planes; determine intersections of lines and planes; find angles.
topics.common_mistakes: Sign errors in cross product (cyclic order); confusing normal vector with direction vector of plane; parametric vs Cartesian form.
topics.remediation: Use the determinant method for cross products. Practise converting between forms of plane equations.

topics.name: Descriptive Statistics
subtopics: Measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode)
Measures of dispersion (range, IQR, standard deviation, variance)
topics.objectives: Collect, organise, and analyse data using statistical measures; calculate and interpret correlation and linear regression.
topics.common_mistakes: Confusing population and sample standard deviation; extrapolating regression beyond data range; misinterpreting r.
topics.remediation: Use technology for calculations but focus on interpreting results in context. Always state conclusions in words.

topics.name: Probability
subtopics: Discrete random variables and expected value
topics.objectives: Calculate probabilities of combined events; use conditional probability; understand discrete probability distributions.
topics.common_mistakes: Confusing independent with mutually exclusive; errors in conditional probability (wrong denominator); forgetting P(A∪B) formula.
topics.remediation: Always draw a diagram (Venn/tree) first. Clearly identify whether events are independent before choosing a formula.

topics.name: Distributions
topics.objectives: Apply binomial and normal distributions; standardise values; find probabilities and quantiles using technology.
topics.common_mistakes: Using normal approximation when n is too small; confusing σ and σ²; not sketching the distribution before calculating.
topics.remediation: Always state parameters (n, p or μ, σ) before calculating. Sketch the distribution and shade the required area.

topics.name: Statistics (AHL)
subtopics: Chi-squared goodness of fit and independence tests
topics.objectives: Apply Bayes' theorem; work with continuous distributions; perform hypothesis tests; use chi-squared tests.
topics.common_mistakes: Confusing null and alternative hypotheses; wrong degrees of freedom; misinterpreting p-values.
topics.remediation: Follow a structured hypothesis testing procedure every time. Practise Bayes' theorem with tree diagrams.

topics.name: Limits & Derivatives
subtopics: Power rule, chain rule, product rule, quotient rule
topics.objectives: Understand limits informally; differentiate using standard rules; find equations of tangent and normal lines.
topics.common_mistakes: Forgetting chain rule on composite functions; sign error in quotient rule; not simplifying before differentiating.
topics.remediation: Practise identifying which rule applies before computing. Show substitution steps clearly for chain rule.

topics.name: Differentiation Applications
topics.objectives: Apply differentiation to find stationary points, solve optimisation and related rates problems.
topics.common_mistakes: Not verifying nature of stationary points; setting up the wrong expression to optimise; sign errors in related rates.
topics.remediation: Practise translating word problems into a function of one variable before differentiating. Always verify nature of critical points.

topics.name: Integration Basics
topics.objectives: Integrate standard functions; evaluate definite integrals; find areas bounded by curves; apply to kinematics.
topics.common_mistakes: Forgetting +C for indefinite integrals; wrong limits or sign when area is below x-axis; not sketching first.
topics.remediation: Always check by differentiating the result. Sketch curves and identify regions above/below axis separately.

topics.name: Calculus Applications
topics.objectives: Apply calculus to model real-world situations involving rates of change, motion, and accumulation.
topics.common_mistakes: Confusing when to differentiate vs integrate; not checking units; forgetting initial conditions.
topics.remediation: Start from the physical context — identify what is changing and whether you need rate (derivative) or total (integral).

topics.name: Calculus AHL: Differentiation
subtopics: Derivatives of trig, exponential, and log functions
topics.objectives: Differentiate transcendental functions; use implicit differentiation; apply L'Hôpital's rule for indeterminate forms.
topics.common_mistakes: Forgetting to differentiate both sides in implicit differentiation; applying L'Hôpital to non-indeterminate forms; chain rule with transcendentals.
topics.remediation: Practise chain rule systematically with each transcendental function. Verify L'Hôpital conditions (0/0 or ∞/∞) before applying.

topics.name: Calculus AHL: Integration
topics.objectives: Apply advanced integration techniques (substitution, by parts); calculate volumes of revolution about x and y axes.
topics.common_mistakes: Wrong substitution choice; forgetting to change limits when substituting; sign errors in integration by parts.
topics.remediation: Use the LIATE rule for choosing u in by-parts. Practise recognising common substitution patterns.

topics.name: Differential Equations
topics.objectives: Solve separable differential equations; interpret slope fields; apply Euler's method; model real-world phenomena with DEs.
topics.common_mistakes: Forgetting the constant of integration when separating variables; errors in Euler's method step calculations; wrong initial conditions.
topics.remediation: Practise separation and integration steps systematically. Draw slope fields to build geometric intuition for solution curves.
